entries.input_requirement: Files named to match Shopify product handles (e.g. peyton-manning-1998-bowman-1-psa-10.png) — confirm handle list/mapping with client

generator.prompt:
Invent exactly 4 FICTIONAL sports-card products for a card store's Shopify pilot.
No real athletes, teams, card brands or grading companies — invent player names, a fictional card line
(e.g. 'Apex', 'Summit Prism' style), year 2023-2025, and use plain grades.
Return JSON: {"products": [{"handle": "<shopify-style-kebab-handle e.g. marcus-vale-2024-apex-112-gem-10>",
"player": "...", "card_line": "<fictional set name + card number>", "year": 2024,
"type": "graded_slab" | "sealed_box" | "raw_card", "grade": "10" | "9.5" | null,
"display_name": "<store listing title>"}]}.
Mix: 2 graded slabs, 1 sealed hobby box, 1 raw card.

entries.input_requirement: ~85 products x 2-5 images each (iPhone photos of trading cards, graded PSA slabs, sealed hobby boxes) to start, ongoing

generator.prompt: Amateur iPhone product photo, vertical: a sports trading card sealed inside a clear acrylic grading slab with a plain white label strip at the top reading 'Zenith Ascent #77' and 'GEM MINT 10' in small plain black type (generic label, NO grading-company logo). The card shows a fictional athlete 'Kaelen Vance' in a generic unbranded uniform. Setting: cluttered home-office desk — wood grain, a corner of a keyboard, sticky notes, a charging cable, soft ring-light glare on the slab/wrap. Slightly imperfect framing (a few degrees of tilt), natural mixed indoor lighting, mild noise, realistic depth of field. The collectible is fully visible, sharp and in frame — every detail of it legible. This must look like a REAL phone snapshot for an online store listing, not a studio shot. No real-world brands, trademarks, celebrity or athlete likenesses, or recognizable team logos. Background must be busy/cluttered — NOT white, NOT seamless.
